name: Southwest Breakfast Burritos
instructions:
1. In a large bowl, whisk together the Eggs, Milk, and Salt until well combined.

2. Heat the Butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat until melted and foaming. Pour in the egg mixture and cook, stirring frequently with a spatula, until the Eggs are completely set and creamy, about 5 minutes. Avoid overcooking.

3. Break up the cooked Eggs into small bite-sized pieces and transfer to a large bowl. Set aside.

4. In the same large skillet over medium heat, add the Bulk Pork Sausage and finely minced Garlic. Cook, breaking up the meat with a wooden spoon, for 5 minutes.

5. Add the diced Red Onion and continue to cook and stir until the sausage is completely crumbly, evenly browned, and no longer pink inside. Drain excess grease.

6. Transfer the sausage mixture to the bowl with the eggs. Stir in the diced Tomato, chopped Fresh Cilantro, diced Jalapeño, and Taco Seasoning, mixing until well combined.

7. Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature, about 15 minutes, then fold in the shredded Cheddar Cheese to distribute evenly.

8. Lay a Tortilla onto your work surface. Spoon a portion of the egg filling onto the tortilla, positioning it halfway between the bottom edge and the center.

9. Fold the bottom of the tortilla snugly over the filling, then fold in the left and right edges toward the center. Roll the burrito firmly up to the top edge, forming a tight, compact cylinder.

10. Repeat the process with the remaining tortillas and filling.

11. Wrap each burrito individually tightly with plastic wrap and store in the freezer until ready to serve. They can be kept for up to 3 months.

12. To serve, unwrap the burritos and heat in the microwave on high until hot throughout, 3 to 4 minutes, turning once halfway through.
simpleInstructions:
1. Whisk the Eggs, Milk, and Salt together, then cook in Butter until set.

2. Cook the Bulk Pork Sausage with minced Garlic, add the diced Red Onion, then mix with the eggs.

3. Fold in the diced Tomato, chopped Fresh Cilantro, diced Jalapeño, Taco Seasoning, and Cheddar Cheese.

4. Fill the Tortillas with the mixture and roll firmly into burritos.

5. Wrap tightly and freeze, then microwave to serve.
